title: Cereus eyriesii
taxonomy: Cereus eyriesii is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. Within the Cactaceae family, it is classified in the subfamily Cactoideae and the tribe Cacteae. The genus Cereus is further divided into several species, with Cereus eyriesii being one of them. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics, such as its stem and flower morphology, as well as its growth habits. The classification of Cereus eyriesii reflects its evolutionary relationships with other cacti species.

side_effects: Cereus eyriesii, also known as the "Rat Tail Cactus", is a species of cactus that is not well-studied in terms of its potential side effects. intro: Cereus erythractus, also known as the Red-Stemmed Cereus, is a species of cactus native to the Americas, belonging to the Echinocereus genus. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cereus erythractus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make topical preparations, such as salves and ointments, as well as infusions, to treat various health conditions.
